goal: Attach an MQTT Sparkplug-B bridge to a local broker (host=127.0.0.1, port=1883) that feeds three industrial IoT data points into Isaac Sim USD attributes: robot_state/joint0_pos (float, radians), conveyor/belt_speed (float, m/s), and cell/estop_active (int, 0/1). Create a minimal USD scene with a Sensor prim that holds these three attributes, attach the MQTT bridge mapping each USD attr path to its Sparkplug-B NDATA topic, verify the bridge is alive via diagnose_mqtt_sparkplug_bridge, then call list_bridges to confirm it appears as kind='mqtt_sparkplug' and alive=True. Exercises the Phase 6 M5 mqtt_sparkplug_bridge_attach + diagnose_mqtt_sparkplug_bridge + list_bridges plumbing in a pure IoT data-feed validation pattern — no robot arm, no pick-place.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-mqtt-sparkplug-iot:

1) Bridge scope: This is the first MQTT Sparkplug-B canonical in the library. It validates the M5 mqtt_sparkplug_bridge_attach plumbing as a pure integration test — no motion planner required. Mirrors the Modbus fixture canonical (CP-NEW-modbus-plc-fixture-io) in structure, making it easy to compare the two bridge patterns.

2) Topic map (3 topics for coverage):
   - spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Robot/joint0_pos  -> /World/Sensor/joint0_pos  (float, radians)
   - spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Conveyor/belt_speed -> /World/Sensor/belt_speed (float, m/s)
   - spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Cell/estop_active   -> /World/Sensor/estop_active (int, 0/1)
   Three semantic types (float/float/int) exercises topic_map type handling.
   Sparkplug B topic format: spBv1.0/<group>/<message_type>/<edge_node_id>/<device_id>

3) Mock broker: A real paho-mqtt broker is not required for schema validation. The bridge attach returns a bridge_id; diagnose confirms the subprocess is alive. The test validates plumbing, not the broker.

4) Detach at end: Exercises the full attach→diagnose→list→detach lifecycle.

5) CPU physics: No GPU dynamics needed — pure bridge canonical.

6) settle_state: Captures bridge_id, alive=True, n_topics=3, kind=mqtt_sparkplug so settle_after_canonical can gate on it.
code:
# ── Minimal scene ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=800.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[10, 10, 1])

# CPU dynamics — no motion planner needed for bridge validation
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Sensor prim with IoT attributes ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Represents a virtual sensor panel: values written by MQTT broker messages.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Sensor", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.05], scale=[0.3, 0.2, 0.1])

# Seed USD attributes — MQTT bridge will overwrite from broker NDATA messages.
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sensor", attr_name="joint0_pos", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sensor", attr_name="belt_speed", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sensor", attr_name="estop_active", value=0)

# ── MQTT Sparkplug-B bridge ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Subscribes to three Sparkplug-B NDATA topics and maps payloads to USD attrs.
# topic format: spBv1.0/<group>/<msg_type>/<edge_node>/<device>
# host=127.0.0.1, port=1883: local Mosquitto / paho mock broker.
bridge_result = mqtt_sparkplug_bridge_attach(
    host="127.0.0.1",
    port=1883,
    topic_map={
        "/World/Sensor/joint0_pos": "spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Robot/joint0_pos",
        "/World/Sensor/belt_speed": "spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Conveyor/belt_speed",
        "/World/Sensor/estop_active": "spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Cell/estop_active",
    },
)
bid = bridge_result["bridge_id"]

# ── Bridge validation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. diagnose_mqtt_sparkplug_bridge: confirm subprocess alive.
status = diagnose_mqtt_sparkplug_bridge(bridge_id=bid)
if not status.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(
        f"MQTT Sparkplug bridge {bid} is not alive after attach. "
        f"last_errors={status.get('last_errors')}"
    )

# 2. list_bridges: confirm our bridge appears as kind=mqtt_sparkplug and alive.
bridges = list_bridges()
match = next((b for b in bridges if b.get("bridge_id") == bid), None)
if match is None:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Bridge {bid} not found in list_bridges output")
if match.get("kind") != "mqtt_sparkplug":
    raise RuntimeError(f"Expected kind='mqtt_sparkplug', got {match.get('kind')}")
if not match.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(f"Bridge {bid} listed but alive=False")

# 3. Detach — exercises full lifecycle.
mqtt_sparkplug_bridge_detach(bridge_id=bid)
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate pending; function-gate pending (requires Mosquitto or paho mock broker on 127.0.0.1:1883)
failure_modes: MQTT broker not running on 127.0.0.1:1883 — bridge subprocess returns alive=False immediately; start with: mosquitto -p 1883 (or: python3 -m paho.mqtt.publish)
Port 1883 blocked by firewall — bridge subprocess TCP connect fails; check 'ss -tlnp | grep 1883'
Sparkplug-B topic string malformed — paho-mqtt silently accepts any string but bridge may fail to parse NDATA payload; topic must follow spBv1.0/<group>/<msg_type>/<edge_node>/<device> pattern
USD attribute type mismatch — estop_active seeded as int 0 but NDATA payload arrives as float; bridge coerces to int; verify set_attribute seed matches expected type
list_bridges kind='mqtt_sparkplug' requires bridge_tools.py to inspect PID file prefix; if subprocess crashes immediately, kind may be missing from output
extension_notes: First MQTT Sparkplug-B canonical in the library. Parallel to CP-NEW-modbus-plc-fixture-io (Modbus) and CP-88 (OpenPLC/Modbus) — completes the three-bridge coverage set. Pure bridge-validation; no robot arm, no pick-place. Sparkplug-B topic format encodes group/edge/device hierarchy explicitly, exercising the topic_map usd_attr→mqtt_topic direction.
code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=800.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[10, 10, 1])

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_prim(prim_path={{sensor_panel.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{sensor_panel.position}}, scale={{sensor_panel.scale}})

set_attribute(prim_path={{sensor_panel.path}}, attr_name="joint0_pos", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path={{sensor_panel.path}}, attr_name="belt_speed", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path={{sensor_panel.path}}, attr_name="estop_active", value=0)

bridge_result = mqtt_sparkplug_bridge_attach(
    host="127.0.0.1",
    port=1883,
    topic_map={
        "{{sensor_panel.path}}/joint0_pos": "spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Robot/joint0_pos",
        "{{sensor_panel.path}}/belt_speed": "spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Conveyor/belt_speed",
        "{{sensor_panel.path}}/estop_active": "spBv1.0/Group1/NDATA/Cell/estop_active",
    },
)
bid = bridge_result["bridge_id"]

status = diagnose_mqtt_sparkplug_bridge(bridge_id=bid)
if not status.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(f"MQTT Sparkplug bridge {bid} not alive. last_errors={status.get('last_errors')}")

bridges = list_bridges()
match = next((b for b in bridges if b.get("bridge_id") == bid), None)
if match is None or match.get("kind") != "mqtt_sparkplug" or not match.get("alive", False):
    raise RuntimeError(f"Bridge {bid} validation failed: {match}")

mqtt_sparkplug_bridge_detach(bridge_id=bid)
